# Data Integrity Checklist: PRSG-008 Layer Planner

**Purpose**: Validate that PRSG-008 requirement text protects the planner's data contract: deterministic dependency ordering, cycle-safe DAG output, source line traceability, checkbox-state fidelity, and worktree-safe repo-relative path normalization.
**Created**: 2026-06-10
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)
**Domain**: data-integrity · **Audience**: PR reviewer · **Depth**: formal release gate

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Deterministic DAG & Increment Ordering

- [ ] CHK001 Is byte-for-byte deterministic planner output stated as a measurable requirement for repeated runs on unchanged input? [Measurability, Spec §FR-003, Spec §SC-001]
- [ ] CHK002 Are `## Dependencies & Execution Order` and `### Incremental Delivery` named as the authoritative ordering sources instead of inferred task prose? [Completeness, Spec §FR-007, Spec §FR-014i]
- [x] CHK003 Is DAG edge derivation, `depends_on` de-duplication/order, and `dependency_cycle.details.cycle` ordering specified deterministically enough for fixtures to assert stable output? [Clarity, Spec §FR-014n, Plan §Phase 1 Design Decisions]
- [ ] CHK004 Are contradictory ordering, unknown increments, empty increments, duplicate increment IDs, and dependency cycles each mapped to stable invalid-plan diagnostic codes? [Completeness, Spec §FR-009, Spec §FR-014g]
- [ ] CHK005 Is `[P]` explicitly prevented from creating its own increment, preserving semantic increment order? [Consistency, Spec §FR-012]

## Source Traceability & Checkbox State

- [ ] CHK006 Are task identifiers, titles, source line numbers, parallel flags, and file/test references required to be preserved in each emitted task? [Completeness, Spec §FR-011, Spec §FR-014d]
- [ ] CHK007 Is source metadata required on increments, tasks, warnings, and errors so diagnostics remain traceable without parsing messages? [Completeness, Spec §FR-014c, Spec §FR-014d, Spec §FR-014e]
- [ ] CHK008 Is checkbox-derived task status constrained to a closed enum (`todo`, `done`) with explicit mapping from unchecked and checked tasks? [Clarity, Spec §FR-014d, Data Model §Task]
- [x] CHK009 Are unsupported or partial checkbox states in task-like lines mapped to a deterministic outcome (`malformed_task`) rather than left ambiguous against the `todo|done` output enum? [Coverage, Spec §FR-014o]
- [ ] CHK010 Is the checkbox-state preservation requirement backed by an explicit fixture/test obligation? [Measurability, Plan §Declared File Operations, Plan §Project Structure]

## Repo-Relative Path Normalization

- [ ] CHK011 Are `feature_dir`, `tasks_file`, task `source.path`, `files`, and `tests` described as repo-relative rather than absolute machine paths? [Clarity, Contract §Envelope, Contract §Task Object]
- [x] CHK012 Is repo-relative normalization specified in a worktree-safe way, including the anchor used to find the worktree root, handling of `./` prefixes, duplicate normalized paths, and out-of-tree references? [Clarity, Spec §FR-014p, Plan §Phase 1 Design Decisions]
- [ ] CHK013 Are missing file/test references modeled as warnings rather than failures after normalization? [Consistency, Spec §FR-013, Spec §FR-014h]
- [ ] CHK014 Are distinct increment-level `files` and `tests` arrays required to be deterministic so repeated runs cannot reorder equivalent reference sets? [Completeness, Spec §FR-014c, Data Model §Increment]

## Diagnostic Data Integrity

- [ ] CHK015 Are warning and error diagnostics required to share one schema shape with code, severity, message, source, and machine-readable details? [Consistency, Spec §FR-014e]
- [ ] CHK016 Are all diagnostic `details` payloads closed per code so fixtures never need to parse human-readable `message` text? [Completeness, Spec §FR-014l]
- [ ] CHK017 Are diagnostic severities schema-consistent so invalid-plan/input-error codes cannot be warnings and warning codes cannot be errors? [Consistency, Spec §FR-014m]
- [ ] CHK018 Are input errors distinct from invalid task-plan content, preserving exit-code and status integrity? [Clarity, Spec §FR-014b, Spec §FR-014k, Spec §FR-015]

## Read-Only & State Integrity

- [ ] CHK019 Is the planner's no-write guarantee stated for successful, warning, invalid-plan, and input-error runs? [Completeness, Spec §FR-005, Spec §SC-004]
- [ ] CHK020 Does autopilot, not the planner, own persistence of successful layer-plan envelopes into `autopilot-state.json` and workflow summaries? [Boundary, Spec §FR-017b, Data Model §AutopilotLayerPlanState]
- [ ] CHK021 Is PRSG-007 routing input consumed only by autopilot, keeping `plan-layers.sh` independent and deterministic on `tasks.md` input? [Boundary, Spec §FR-016, Plan §Summary]
- [ ] CHK022 Is PRSG-009 branch/PR emission explicitly deferred so planner output cannot mutate repository topology? [Scope, Spec §FR-019, Spec §Assumptions]

## Fixture & Acceptance Coverage

- [ ] CHK023 Can valid fixtures assert the full layer-plan envelope, ordered increments, embedded tasks, source lines, checkbox status, and advisory counts without manual inspection? [Measurability, Spec §FR-020, Spec §SC-001, Spec §SC-006]
- [ ] CHK024 Can malformed fixtures assert missing headings, empty increments, dependency cycles, unknown increments, duplicate IDs, contradictory order, and malformed task-like lines? [Coverage, Spec §FR-009, Spec §FR-014g]
- [ ] CHK025 Can warning fixtures assert task-without-reference and missing-reference diagnostics while still receiving `status=ok` and exit `0`? [Coverage, Spec §FR-013, Spec §SC-005]
- [ ] CHK026 Can input-error fixtures assert invocation/path errors, structured JSON, exit `2`, and no repository writes? [Coverage, Spec §FR-014k, Spec §SC-004]
- [ ] CHK027 Can deterministic output tests catch path-order, dependency-order, and file-enumeration drift rather than only checking that output is valid JSON? [Measurability, Spec §SC-001, Plan §Testing]

## Notes

- Focus areas selected: deterministic and cycle-safe dependency DAG; preservation of source line numbers and checkbox state; repo-relative path normalization across worktrees.
- Defaults applied from checklist workflow: reviewer audience, formal release-gate depth.
- Remediation: CHK003 is resolved by Spec FR-014n and the plan's DAG normalization decision.
- Remediation: CHK009 is resolved by Spec FR-014o, which maps unsupported checkbox states to `malformed_task`.
- Remediation: CHK012 is resolved by Spec FR-014p and the plan's worktree-root normalization decision.
